failure_modes.text: A mental model becomes dangerous when it is treated as the territory rather than as a partial map.
failure_modes.mitigation: Label every model as a map, not the territory: require the team to name at least one important thing the model leaves out before using it to decide.

failure_modes.text: Availability-driven substitution makes the team reuse a simple familiar model instead of the more complicated one actually required.
failure_modes.source_quote: substituting a simple model we know for the complicated model actually required
failure_modes.mitigation: Rotate models deliberately: when availability keeps surfacing the same frame, force a second pass with a structurally different model to expose what the familiar one hides.

failure_modes.text: Cognitive entrenchment leads experts to skip details and protect the worldview they already carry.
failure_modes.mitigation: Schedule periodic model audits where experts must explain, not just assert, the mechanisms — surfacing where their shortcuts have become stale.

premortem_questions.text: What repeated surprise or prediction failure is telling us that our current map is missing the real structure?
premortem_questions.source_quote: repeated surprise, disagreement, or prediction failure

premortem_questions.text: What mechanism, threshold, or protocol would follow if this worldview were actually true?
premortem_questions.source_quote: what specific mechanism, threshold, or protocol would follow from it

premortem_questions.text: Which outside-view fact would force us to update this model of reality rather than defend it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: what is true generally of the world independent of one's perspective

heuristics.text: Compare competing mental models explicitly and choose the one that predicts, explains, and guides action best.
Use anti-goals and inversion to reduce error before optimizing for brilliance.
Translate complex ideas into simple explanations or analogies to reveal where the model still has gaps.

curation_notes.summary: Chunk set stays close to the source's strongest failure modes, premortem questions, and repeatable heuristics.
donor_drops: Did not widen the chunk surface beyond clearly source-backed failure, premortem, and heuristic material.
open_questions: Some chunk text is compressed for operational reuse, but each item stays traceable to explicit source language.

allies: Relation-rich ally material was left for Wave 3 instead of being smuggled into chunk text.
antagonists: Bias and conflict material stronger as first-order relations was deferred to Wave 3.
structured_tensions: Any higher-order candidacy remains observational only in batch artifacts.
